events.label: assistant
events.text: Findings written to `/app/answer.json`. Summary: over-greasing that purges a seal is a genuine defect, not just a cosmetic excess — the pressure forces the seal lip out of its correct sealing geometry, so the seal is mechanically compromised even after wiping the excess grease off. Verdict is `fail` / `high` risk with `installed-defective` condition; corrective path is to stop relubrication, replace the damaged seal per SKF/Timken fit guidance, and redo lubrication with a calibrated/metered grease gun to OEM-specified quantity (SKF's G ≈ 0.005 × D × B formula) using any relief/purge port rather than greasing until purge is visible.
